label: Neutral Stand
description: Relaxed upright stance, feet hip-width apart, arms hanging naturally at the sides. The foundation of all figure drawing.
tips: Observe the natural S-curve of the spine
Shoulders sit directly above the hips
Weight is distributed evenly on both feet

label: T-Pose
description: Arms extended horizontally at shoulder height, palms down, feet shoulder-width apart. Classic reference for proportions.
tips: Arms should be perfectly level — use the shoulder line as a guide
Check that the torso length equals roughly 3 head-heights
Wrists align with the shoulder plane

label: Front View Stand
description: Full-body frontal view, arms slightly away from the body. Essential for studying bilateral symmetry and proportions.
tips: Draw a vertical centre line from nose to feet
Check that left and right sides mirror each other
Note the slight inward angle of the lower legs

label: Side Profile Stand
description: Pure lateral view showing the natural spinal curves, chest projection, and leg alignment from the side.
tips: The ear, shoulder, hip, knee and ankle should align vertically
Notice the forward curve of the lumbar spine
The chest projects forward of the shoulder line

label: A-Pose
description: Arms angled down at roughly 45°, feet slightly apart. A relaxed variant of the T-pose used in character rigging.
tips: Arms form a 45° angle with the torso
Fingers are relaxed, not splayed
Weight is centred between both feet

label: Running Stride
description: Mid-stride running pose with strong diagonal lines through the body. Great for studying dynamic weight shift.
tips: The torso leans forward — find the angle of the spine
Opposite arm and leg move together
Look for the compression on the landing side

label: Jump — Peak
description: Full-body jump at peak height, limbs extended. Captures weightlessness and extreme foreshortening.
tips: The centre of gravity is at its highest — draw it first
Limbs radiate outward from the core
Foreshortening affects the legs most — observe carefully

label: Reaching Up
description: One arm fully extended overhead, body slightly stretched to the side. Shows ribcage lift and lateral stretch.
tips: The ribcage tilts toward the raised arm
The hip on the raised-arm side drops slightly
Feel the stretch from hip to fingertip as a single line

label: Torso Twist
description: Shoulders rotated relative to hips, creating a spiral through the spine. Core gesture drawing exercise.
tips: Draw the shoulder line and hip line as separate angles
The spine creates an S-curve in the twist
The further the twist, the more the ribcage compresses on one side

label: Lunge Forward
description: Deep forward lunge, front knee at 90°, back leg extended. Strong diagonal energy through the body.
tips: Front knee should not pass the toes
The torso stays upright — check the vertical spine
Back heel lifts — observe the angle of the rear foot

label: Side Kick
description: One leg raised laterally to hip height, arms in guard position. Extreme weight shift onto one leg.
tips: The standing leg carries all the weight — draw it strong
The raised hip is higher than the standing hip
The torso counter-leans away from the kick

label: Sitting — Upright
description: Seated on a surface, spine straight, feet flat on the floor. Studies the foreshortening of the thighs.
tips: Thighs foreshorten significantly — they appear shorter than they are
The pelvis tilts slightly forward when sitting upright
Knees are at roughly 90° — check the angle carefully

label: Sitting — Relaxed Lean
description: Seated with a relaxed backward lean, one arm supporting. Shows the collapsed S-curve of a resting spine.
tips: The spine rounds when leaning back — no longer straight
The supporting arm creates a strong diagonal
The shoulders drop and roll forward slightly

label: Cross-Legged Floor Sit
description: Seated on the floor with legs crossed, hands resting on knees. Challenges the drawing of foreshortened legs.
tips: The pelvis tilts backward — the lower back rounds
Knees are wide and low — observe the diamond shape of the legs
The feet tuck under the opposite knee

label: Seated Forward Fold
description: Seated with legs extended, torso folded forward over the legs. Extreme spinal flexion and hamstring stretch.
tips: The spine curves in a long arc from tailbone to neck
The head hangs heavy — it is not lifted
The legs stay straight — observe the angle of the feet

label: Contrapposto
description: Classical weight-shift pose: weight on one leg, hip raised on that side, opposite shoulder raised. The foundation of naturalistic figure drawing.
tips: Hip and shoulder lines tilt in opposite directions
The weight-bearing leg is straight, the free leg is bent
The spine creates a gentle S-curve between the two tilts

label: Power Stance
description: Feet wider than shoulders, slight knee bend, hands on hips. Emphasises the width of the pelvis and shoulder girdle.
tips: The trapezoid of the torso is widest at the shoulders
The pelvis is the widest point of the lower body
Both feet are planted — equal weight distribution

label: Arms Overhead Stretch
description: Both arms raised fully overhead, slight back arch, chin lifted. Shows the full length of the torso and ribcage lift.
tips: The ribcage lifts and expands — it is no longer compressed
The waist narrows as the obliques stretch
The arms align with the ears, not in front of them

label: Wide Side Lunge
description: Wide lateral lunge, one knee bent to 90°, other leg straight. Emphasises the adductor muscles and hip width.
tips: The bent knee tracks directly over the foot
The straight leg is fully extended — no bend at the knee
The torso stays upright between the two legs

label: Back View Stand
description: Full-body rear view, arms slightly away from the body. Essential for studying the back muscles, spine, and gluteal structure.
tips: The spine is visible as a central groove between the erector muscles
The shoulder blades (scapulae) are prominent landmarks
The gluteal crease defines the bottom of the torso

label: Three-Quarter View
description: 45° rotated view showing both front and side planes of the body simultaneously. The most common view in figure drawing.
tips: The centre line of the torso curves around the form
One shoulder is closer — it appears larger
The far hip is partially hidden by the near hip

label: Warrior I
description: Deep lunge with back foot at 45°, arms raised overhead, hips squared to the front. Strong diagonal energy.
tips: Front knee stacks directly over the ankle
Back leg is straight and strong — feel the tension
Arms reach up alongside the ears, not in front

label: Tree Balance
description: Single-leg balance with raised foot pressed to inner thigh, hands in prayer. Tests understanding of the balance axis.
tips: The entire body weight passes through one ankle
The standing hip is slightly raised — not level
The spine stays vertical — resist the lean

label: Squat — Deep
description: Full depth squat, heels flat, arms forward for balance. Extreme hip and knee flexion — challenging foreshortening.
tips: The torso leans forward to counterbalance the hips
The thighs are nearly horizontal — heavily foreshortened
The knees track over the toes — observe the angle

label: Crescent Lunge
description: Deep lunge with back knee lifted, arms raised overhead, slight backbend. Full-body extension with complex curves.
tips: The back hip tucks under — do not let it sag
The spine arches gently backward from the thoracic region
Arms reach up and slightly back — follow the arc
